prompt: A colleague is running Beagle 5.4 imputation on 480 GBS rice samples using the 3K indica reference panel. The imputation completes without errors, but the output shows suspicious results: DR2 values are uniformly high (mean 0.92) across all MAF bins including rare variants (MAF 0.01-0.05), and a PCA of imputed genotypes shows all 480 GBS samples collapsing into a single tight cluster with no visible substructure, despite the samples coming from 6 different breeding programs across 4 countries. Diagnose the most likely cause and propose a fix.
context:
Beagle 5.4 command used:
  java -jar beagle.5.4.jar \
    gt=gbs_480_samples.vcf.gz \
    ref=indica_1354_ref.vcf.gz \
    map=rice_genetic_map.map \
    out=chr01_imputed \
    nthreads=8

Genetic map file (first 5 lines of rice_genetic_map.map):
  chr01 . 0.0 11075
  chr01 . 0.132 52811
  chr01 . 0.274 103948
  chr01 . 0.418 152307
  chr01 . 0.551 205822

Beagle map format specification (from documentation):
  Column 1: chromosome
  Column 2: SNP ID (or .)
  Column 3: genetic position in centiMorgans
  Column 4: physical position in base pairs

PLINK map format:
  Column 1: chromosome
  Column 2: SNP ID
  Column 3: genetic position in centiMorgans
  Column 4: physical position in base pairs

Output statistics:
  Total imputed variants: 11,842,106
  Mean DR2 across all variants: 0.92
  Mean DR2 for MAF 0.01-0.05: 0.89
  Mean DR2 for MAF 0.05-0.20: 0.93
  Mean DR2 for MAF 0.20-0.50: 0.94
  PCA of imputed genotypes: all 480 samples in single cluster
  Het rate of imputed genotypes: 0.008 (vs 0.06-0.08 expected for indica)

Reference panel statistics:
  indica panel mean heterozygosity: 0.072
  indica panel mean DR2 from leave-one-out validation: 0.81 (MAF 0.01-0.05), 0.94 (MAF>0.20)
correct_answer:
The root cause is NOT a genetic map format error (the map appears to use the correct Beagle format). The real problem is that the GENETIC MAP HAS NEAR-ZERO RECOMBINATION RATES -- the map values are far too compressed.

DIAGNOSIS:
Looking at the genetic map: physical positions span 11,075 to 205,822 bp (~195 kb) but genetic positions only span 0.0 to 0.551 cM. This gives a recombination rate of ~0.28 cM/100kb, which is actually within the normal range for rice (~0.3-0.4 cM/100kb genome-wide average). So the map itself is not obviously wrong in rate.

The ACTUAL problem is the combination of symptoms:
1. UNIFORMLY HIGH DR2 across all MAF bins (rare variants at 0.89 when leave-one-out validation gives 0.81) -- this means Beagle is overconfident in its imputation. Imputed dosages are being pushed toward 0 or 2 (homozygous) rather than reflecting uncertainty.

2. EXTREMELY LOW HETEROZYGOSITY (0.008 vs expected 0.06-0.08) -- Beagle is imputing almost entirely homozygous genotypes. While rice is a selfing species with high homozygosity, a het rate of 0.008 is ~10x lower than expected.

3. ALL SAMPLES COLLAPSING TO ONE CLUSTER -- imputation is producing nearly identical genotypes for all 480 samples, obliterating real genetic differences between breeding programs.

The most likely cause is that the GBS INPUT VCF has incorrect or missing genotype likelihoods (GL/PL fields). If the input VCF contains only hard-called GT fields with no uncertainty information, Beagle treats all observed genotypes as certain and imputes missing sites purely from the reference panel haplotype frequencies. At 2.8x GBS depth, most sites have only 1-2 reads, which is insufficient for reliable genotype calling. If these are hard-called as homozygous reference (the default for low-depth callers when few reads are present), Beagle sees most GBS sites as confidently homozygous-ref and imputes unobserved sites by extending those haplotypes -- producing uniformly homozygous, high-confidence output that is essentially copying the most common reference haplotype.

FIX:
(a) Re-call genotypes from raw GBS reads using a likelihood-based caller (e.g., bcftools mpileup with --annotate FORMAT/PL) that emits genotype likelihoods, not hard calls.
(b) Provide the GL/PL-annotated VCF to Beagle so it can incorporate genotype uncertainty into the HMM.
(c) Alternatively, use GLIMPSE2 instead of Beagle -- GLIMPSE is specifically designed for low-coverage data and handles genotype uncertainty natively from BAM files.
(d) After re-imputation, verify that DR2 for rare variants drops to ~0.75-0.85 (appropriate uncertainty) and that PCA recovers breeding program substructure.
rubric: Full marks (3): Must identify that the core issue is missing/incorrect genotype likelihoods in the GBS input (not a map format error), explain why hard-called low-coverage genotypes produce overconfident homozygous imputation, connect all three symptoms (high DR2, low het, collapsed PCA) to this single root cause, and recommend likelihood-based calling or GLIMPSE2. Mostly correct (2): Correctly identifies the GL/PL issue as a likely cause and proposes a fix, but does not fully explain the mechanism connecting all three symptoms, OR identifies a different but plausible root cause (e.g., reference panel mismatch) with good reasoning. Partial (1): Notes that something is wrong with the input data but misdiagnoses as a map format error or reference panel issue without engaging with the symptom pattern. Incorrect (0): Concludes that the results are correct (rice is selfing so low het is expected) or focuses only on the genetic map format. Major error: Recommending to simply lower the DR2 threshold or re-run with more threads.
notes: This is a trap item. The genetic map LOOKS like it might be in the wrong format (it superficially resembles PLINK format), but it is actually correct. The real issue is more subtle: missing genotype likelihoods in low-coverage GBS data. Models that fixate on the map format will miss the actual diagnosis.
